prompt_text: Best product feedback platforms for collect and prioritize customer feedback to inform product roadmap decisions
response:
If your goal is to **collect, organize, and prioritize customer feedback for roadmap decisions**, the strongest options in the results are **UserVoice**, **Pylon**, **Qualaroo**, **Survicate**, and **Lyssna**.[1][3][5][7] The best fit depends on whether you need **feedback management and prioritization**, **support-to-product workflow**, or **survey-driven research**.[1][5][7]

| Platform | Best for | Why it stands out |
|---|---|---|
| **UserVoice** | **Feedback management & prioritization** | Built for structured intake, voting, triage, and roadmap decisions based on customer input.[1] |
| **Pylon** | **Support + product decision workflows** | Connects customer feedback from support conversations to product decisions across channels.[5] |
| **Qualaroo** | **In-app feedback collection** | Strong for targeted microsurveys and real-time UX/product feedback inside web or app experiences.[1][4] |
| **Survicate** | **Multi-channel surveys** | Supports surveys via email, web, in-product, and mobile apps for broad feedback capture.[7] |
| **Lyssna** | **All-in-one product research** | Useful across the product lifecycle, from concepts to post-launch feedback.[3] |

For **roadmap prioritization specifically**, **UserVoice** is the clearest match because it emphasizes a structured feedback pipeline, user voting, and PM decision-making.[1] If you want to turn customer support input into product actions, **Pylon** is a strong choice because it is positioned around organizing feedback across channels and connecting it to product decisions.[5]

For **collecting feedback at the moment of use**, **Qualaroo** is a good fit because it captures real-time user insights through targeted in-app microsurveys.[1][4] If you need **broader survey distribution** across multiple channels, **Survicate** is better suited to that use case.[7]

A practical way to choose:

- Choose **UserVoice** if you need a formal **feature request and prioritization system**.[1]
- Choose **Pylon** if you want feedback tied closely to **support and customer conversations**.[5]
- Choose **Qualaroo** if you want **in-product microsurveys** for UX and feature feedback.[1][4]
- Choose **Survicate** if you need **multi-channel survey collection**.[7]
- Choose **Lyssna** if you want a broader **product research** platform across discovery and launch stages.[3]

If you want, I can also narrow these down to the **best 3 for SaaS teams**, **best for startups**, or **best budget options**.
